title: Rich Spanish Black Treacle Ensaимada
description: A bitter and indulgent Spanish dessert featuring black treacle, inspired by Andalusian cuisine, with a mortar-pestle-pound preparation method, suitable for an ayurvedic-rajasic diet.
instructions: In a mortar, pound the sugar and salt until well combined.
Add the black treacle to the mortar and mix until smooth.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and yeast.
Add the warm water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Add the melted butter and eggs to the bowl and mix until the dough is smooth.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and shape into a spiral ensaимada shape.
Place the ensaимada on a baking sheet and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before serving.
